GA4 Web Analytics B2C data product, providing market‑level performance metrics aggregated across web and app channels. It captures monthly and year‑to‑date (YTD) insights on user traffic, engagement, and e‑commerce behavior by geography, market, platform, device, channel, and time, enabling business users to evaluate digital performance and conversion effectiveness across markets.

values: Source system primary key(made from concatination of the dimensional columns) of the table. (TABLE_KEY)

Cart Abandonment Rate (also known as Shopping Cart Abandonment Rate) measures the percentage of sessions where users add one or more items to their shopping cart but leave the site or app without completing the purchase. (CART_ABANDONMENT_RATE_MONTHLY)

Cart Abandonment Rate (also known as Shopping Cart Abandonment Rate) measures the percentage of sessions where users add one or more items to their shopping cart but leave the site or app without completing the purchase. (CART_ABANDONMENT_RATE_YTD)
Cart Conversion Rate is the percentage of  sessions where an item added to the cart ultimately results in a completed purchase. (CART_CONVERSION_RATE_MONTHLY)
Cart Conversion Rate is the percentage of  sessions where an item added to the cart ultimately results in a completed purchase. (CART_CONVERSION_RATE_YTD)

An Engaged Session in GA4 is a session that meets any of the following criteria:
1. Lasts 10 seconds or more, OR
2. Has 1 or more conversion events, OR
3. Has 2 or more screen or pageviews (ENGAGED_SESSIONS_MONTHLY)

An Engaged Session in GA4 is a session that meets any of the following criteria:
1. Lasts 10 seconds or more, OR
2. Has 1 or more conversion events, OR
3. Has 2 or more screen or pageviews (ENGAGED_SESSIONS_YTD)

Engagement Rate is the percentage of sessions on your website that are considered "engaged."
A session is classified as engaged when a user:
1. Actively stays on the site for at least 10 seconds, or
2. Views 2 or more pages or screens, or
3. Triggers a conversion event (such as a purchase, sign-up, etc.) (ENGAGEMENT_RATE_MONTHLY)

Engagement Rate is the percentage of sessions on your website that are considered "engaged."
A session is classified as engaged when a user:
1. Actively stays on the site for at least 10 seconds, or
2. Views 2 or more pages or screens, or
3. Triggers a conversion event (such as a purchase, sign-up, etc.) (ENGAGEMENT_RATE_YTD)
It Represents the timestamp when a record is created or last updated. (UPDATED_AT)
Total number of unique user sessions across the site or app. A session starts when a user engages and ends after 30 minutes of inactivity. (TOTAL_SESSIONS_MONTHLY)
Total number of unique user sessions across the site or app. A session starts when a user engages and ends after 30 minutes of inactivity. (TOTAL_SESSIONS_YTD)
Total number of unique users who interacts with your website or app. (USERS_VISIT_MONTHLY)
Total number of unique users who interacts with your website or app. (USERS_VISIT_YTD)
The User Purchase Ratio shows the percentage of users who made a purchase out of all users in a given period. (USER_PURCHASE_RATIO_MONTHLY)
The User Purchase Ratio shows the percentage of users who made a purchase out of all users in a given period. (USER_PURCHASE_RATIO_YTD)
It represents the month\'s end date. (YEAR_TO_DATE)
